core_mechanism: adiabatic (slowly-varying-parameter) evolution of a time-dependent linear operator whose success is bottlenecked by an isolated Landau-Zener avoided crossing of its two lowest eigenvalues, with the minimum spectral gap setting the required evolution timescale and the finite-size scaling of that gap determining polynomial-vs-exponential complexity
brief_reason: The abstract names the algorithm's bottleneck as an isolated Landau-Zener-type avoided crossing between the two lowest energy levels and studies how the minimum gap's size-dependence (N <= 128) yields polynomial rather than exponential median cost.
